why: Guards whether enough input bytes remain to hold the long-form length's declared number of octets, the most fundamental DER length-field validity check; an LLM can craft a truncated long-form length (e.g. 0x84 followed by fewer than 4 bytes) to flip it.

Checks that a definite-length TLV's declared content length plus header does not exceed the bytes actually available in the buffer, the core over-length/truncation rejection for every nested ASN.1 field; craftable by declaring a length larger than the remaining bytes.

Bounds-checks a decoded universal tag (>30, i.e. reached via high-tag-number form) before indexing the tag2bit table used by MSTRING/CHOICE types like GENERAL_NAME's string alternatives; an LLM can emit a high-tag-number-encoded tag byte to exercise this out-of-range path.
Verifies the decoded tag equals V_ASN1_OBJECT (universal OID tag 0x06) before accepting the field as an OBJECT IDENTIFIER; craftable by substituting any other tag byte on an OID-typed field.

Enforces X.690 8.19.2 OID content sanity (non-zero length, final subidentifier octet's MSB clear) before accepting the OID bytes; craftable by giving zero-length OID content or setting the high bit on the last content octet.

Selects the strict RFC 5280 X.509 minimum-length/format requirement for GeneralizedTime (seconds mandatory, Zulu-only); craftable by producing a GeneralizedTime value in a certificate-time context that is short/lenient rather than always taking the lenient branch.

Decides, in non-strict mode, whether the seconds field of a UTCTime/GeneralizedTime is actually present as two digits versus elided in favor of an immediate timezone marker; craftable by supplying a full HHMMSS time string instead of always eliding seconds.

Rejects fractional-seconds notation in a GeneralizedTime when RFC 5280 strict mode is active, per the spec's ban on fractional seconds; craftable by adding a '.'-plus-digits fractional field to a strict-mode GeneralizedTime.
